An update from Dongyue Group Limited ( (HK:0189) ) is now available.

Dongyue Group Limited has called its annual general meeting for 4 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will vote on routine governance matters including adoption of the 2025 audited financial statements, re-election of two executive directors and one independent non-executive director, and authorization for the board to set directors’ and auditors’ remuneration. The agenda also includes re-appointment of Zhonghui Anda CPA Limited as auditor, declaration of a final dividend of HK$0.30 per share for 2025, and renewal of a general mandate allowing the board to issue up to 20 percent of the company’s share capital, moves that collectively shape capital management, corporate oversight and shareholder returns for the coming year.

More about Dongyue Group Limited

Dongyue Group Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and traded under stock code 189. It operates as a diversified industrial group, with its shares listed on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong, and is overseen by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors.

data.title: Tsingtao Brewery Director Resigns, Triggering Board Committee Changes
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Tsingtao Brewery Co ( (HK:0168) ) is now available.

Tsingtao Brewery announced the resignation of independent non-executive director Sheng Lei Ming, who has served since June 2020 and is stepping down as his six-year term approaches under relevant regulatory requirements. Sheng is also resigning as chairman of the Nomination & Remuneration Committee and as a member of the Audit & Internal Control Committee, and he has confirmed there is no disagreement with the board or matters needing shareholder attention.

His departure leaves the Nomination & Remuneration Committee without a chair, putting the brewer temporarily out of compliance with Hong Kong listing rules that require this role to be filled by an independent non-executive director. The company plans to appoint a suitable replacement within three months to restore compliance and will issue a further announcement, while the board publicly thanked Sheng for his contributions during his tenure.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0168) stock is a Buy with a HK$67.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Tsingtao Brewery Co stock, see the HK:0168 Stock Forecast page.

More about Tsingtao Brewery Co

Tsingtao Brewery Company Limited is a major Chinese beer producer operating as a Sino-foreign joint stock company listed in Hong Kong. The group focuses on brewing and distributing beer and related beverages, serving both domestic and international markets under the well-known Tsingtao brand.

Average Trading Volume: 3,932,733

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.title: Wai Kee to Replace Deloitte With Crowe as Auditor After 33 Years
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Wai Kee Holdings Limited ( (HK:0610) ) has shared an update.

Wai Kee Holdings Limited has announced a planned change of auditor, with Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu set to retire after the conclusion of the annual general meeting on 22 May 2026, ending more than 33 years in the role. The board and audit committee, citing auditor rotation as being in the best interests of shareholders, will propose the appointment of Crowe (HK) CPA Limited as the new auditor, subject to shareholder approval and completion of Crowe’s client acceptance procedures.

In selecting Crowe, the audit committee assessed audit fees, industry knowledge, independence, proposed audit services, technical competence, capacity and compliance with Hong Kong regulatory guidelines, and concluded Crowe is suitable for the mandate. The company and Deloitte both confirmed there is no disagreement between them, aside from issues previously disclosed in a modified audit opinion related to the going concern of Road King Infrastructure Limited and its restructuring, and a circular detailing the proposed appointment and AGM notice will be sent to shareholders in due course.

More about Wai Kee Holdings Limited

Wai Kee Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong–listed company incorporated in Bermuda and traded under stock code 610. It operates as an investment holding group with interests including infrastructure-related businesses through subsidiaries such as Road King Infrastructure Limited, focusing on projects that are subject to Hong Kong financial reporting and listing regulations.

The company is overseen by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors, supported by an audit committee that evaluates auditor performance, independence and suitability. Its operations and governance are closely aligned with the requirements of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong and the Accounting and Financial Reporting Council guidelines.

Average Trading Volume: 82,781

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$729.7M

Anhui Conch Cement Company ( (HK:0914) ) just unveiled an update.

Anhui Conch Cement has declared a final ordinary dividend of RMB 0.61 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, with shareholder approval scheduled for 28 May 2026. The dividend for H shareholders will be paid in Hong Kong dollars on 30 June 2026, with the exact HKD amount and exchange rate to be announced, and an ex-dividend date set for 2 June 2026.

The company detailed key Hong Kong share register dates, including the last time to lodge transfer documents on 3 June and a book closure from 4 to 10 June 2026, to determine dividend entitlements. It also outlined the withholding tax treatment for different categories of shareholders, specifying a 10% rate for non-resident enterprises and individuals outside the PRC and a 20% rate for mainland individual investors accessing H shares via Stock Connect, clarifying after-tax returns for various investor groups.

data.title: SEA Holdings Sells Shouson Hill Property for HK$180 Million
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

SEA Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:0251) ) has shared an announcement.

SEA Holdings has agreed to dispose of a luxury residential property at No. 1 Shouson Hill Road East, Hong Kong, via the sale of a wholly owned holding structure for HK$180 million in cash, subject to completion adjustments. The deal, structured through the sale of the share and shareholder loan in the property-owning vehicle, transfers full beneficial ownership of the asset to an independent third-party purchaser.

The consideration, set after arm’s length negotiations and with reference to comparable market values and prevailing Hong Kong conditions, will be paid in stages with deposits held in stakeholder until the existing mortgage can be fully discharged. As the transaction size falls between 5% and 25% under Hong Kong listing rules, it is classified as a discloseable transaction, requiring market notification but not shareholder approval, signalling a portfolio rebalancing move rather than a transformational change for SEA Holdings.

More about SEA Holdings Ltd.

SEA Holdings Ltd., incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the property and real estate sector. The group invests in, develops and holds properties, with a portfolio that includes residential and other real estate assets in Hong Kong and potentially other markets, positioning it as a regional property investment and development company.

data.title: Ping An Healthcare Delivers Strong Q1 2026 Profit Growth on Insurance Synergy and AI-Driven Health Services
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ping An Healthcare and Technology Company ( (HK:1833) ) just unveiled an update.

Ping An Healthcare and Technology Company reported first-quarter 2026 revenue of RMB1.16 billion, up 9.1% year on year, with net profit more than doubling to RMB79.1 million and adjusted net profit rising 45.8% to RMB84.4 million. Management attributed the performance to stronger integration with Ping An Group’s insurance business and rapid expansion in corporate health management.

The company deepened mutual empowerment with Ping An Group by embedding enhanced membership health services into insurance products to boost product appeal and scenario-based marketing. In corporate health management, paying corporate clients over the past 12 months surpassed 7,500, nearly 89% higher year on year, while GMV continued to grow.

Ping An Healthcare expanded its “online, in-hospital, in-home, and in-company” service system, with AI Doctor users exceeding 5.6 million and its QR code payment network for offline pharmacy purchases reaching 111,000 outlets nationwide. Home-based senior care users climbed above 290,000, and new workplace clinics were added to improve employees’ on-site service experience.

The Group also strengthened its AI capabilities, using a combined “AI + real doctors” approach to improve medical service quality and efficiency, with complex disease treatment plans from its MDT platform achieving about a 95% accuracy rate. These advances are intended to differentiate its offerings and support broader application of AI in professional medical services and product innovation within its “insurance + health care” model.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1833) stock is a Hold with a HK$12.40 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ping An Healthcare and Technology Company stock, see the HK:1833 Stock Forecast page.

More about Ping An Healthcare and Technology Company

Ping An Healthcare and Technology Company Limited, listed in Hong Kong, operates in the digital healthcare and health management industry, focusing on an “insurance + health care” synergistic model. The Group provides online medical services, corporate health management, and scenario-based health solutions, targeting individual users, insurers and enterprise clients across China.

Investors will vote on the re-election of three executive directors and one independent non-executive director, as well as the authorization of the board to set directors’ pay and the re-appointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor. The meeting will further ask shareholders to renew a general mandate allowing the board to issue up to 20% of the company’s issued share capital, excluding treasury shares, which would provide additional flexibility for future capital-raising or strategic transactions.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2283) stock is a Buy with a HK$3.04 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on TK Group (Holdings) Ltd. stock, see the HK:2283 Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Genertec Universal Medical Unit to Issue RMB500 Million Onshore Notes to Refinance Debt
data.description:

Genertec Universal Medical Group Company ( (HK:2666) ) has shared an announcement.

Genertec Universal Medical Group said its wholly owned subsidiary, China Universal Leasing, plans to issue the first tranche of its onshore medium-term notes for 2026 in China’s interbank bond market. The notes, with an aggregate principal of up to RMB500 million, a two-year tenor and a fixed interest rate to be set via bookbuilding, will be offered to qualified domestic institutional investors.

The issuer holds an approved programme of up to RMB3 billion, of which RMB2.5 billion remains available, and intends to use the net proceeds from this tranche to repay maturing debt. The notes are expected to start trading on the PRC interbank bond market from April 27, 2026, with China CITIC Bank and Postal Savings Bank of China acting as joint lead underwriters, highlighting the group’s continued access to China’s onshore funding channels and efforts to manage its liability profile.

More about Genertec Universal Medical Group Company

Genertec Universal Medical Group Company Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated healthcare group with operations in mainland China. Through its wholly owned subsidiary China Universal Leasing Co., Ltd., the group engages in financial leasing and related financing services to institutional clients, supporting the healthcare and related sectors in the Chinese market.

Celestial Asia Securities Holdings ( (HK:1049) ) has issued an announcement.

Celestial Asia Securities Holdings has convened its annual general meeting for 29 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and consider the re-election of five retiring directors. The meeting will also seek shareholder approval to reappoint Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu as auditor and to grant the board a general mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s existing share capital, excluding treasury shares, providing the company with flexibility to raise equity capital and manage its capital structure over the coming year.

If approved, the re-election of the incumbent directors will ensure continuity of the existing leadership team and corporate strategy. The proposed share issuance mandate, a common practice for Hong Kong-listed companies, would enhance the company’s ability to respond quickly to fundraising opportunities and market conditions, which may affect future dilution levels and capital management options for shareholders.

More about Celestial Asia Securities Holdings

Celestial Asia Securities Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in Bermuda, operating in the financial services sector. It is involved in securities-related businesses and serves investors in the Hong Kong market, with its shares traded on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong under stock code 1049.
